literal: integral / full non-duality; pūrṇa (full, complete, integral) + advaita (non-duality)
invariant_definition: Pūrṇa-Advaita ("integral non-duality") names the modern Vedāntic position most associated with Sri Aurobindo (1872–1950), as distinct from Śaṅkara's classical Advaita. It affirms the non-dual reality of Brahman but insists this reality is full or integral (pūrṇa): the world and its evolution are not an illusion to be negated but a real self-expression and progressive manifestation of the Divine. Where classical Advaita treats liberation as withdrawal from a sublatable plurality, Pūrṇa-Advaita holds matter, life, and mind to be real movements of consciousness (cit) descending and ascending, with the goal a divinization ("supramentalization") of embodied existence rather than escape from it.
